entry.title: ME55 release option rejection SAP
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to rejection Purchase Requisition Number with ME55. How can I do this process?

entry.title: Re: Bottleneck work center to multiple operations
entry.content: Hi, The scrap you input is the operation scrap which does not reflect to the scrap in the production order header. The scrap in the production order header is the assembly scrap. The assembly scrap will add your order quantity. For example, you want to produce 100 quantity assembly material, you need to set the order quantity as 102 if there is 2% scrap portion. Means that you will scrap 2 quantity.

entry.title: Multiple counter plan order not being generated
entry.content:
Hello! I am currently working on a multiple counter plan , when i schedule the plan after i pass the first cycle , an order is generated but as shown below when  i pass the second cycle no order is generated 

I used a cycle  : sequence 1 : 10000 km / 30 days   and sequence 2 : 30000 km / 40 days

Can someone explain to me why and what i am doing wrong ?

Thank you!

entry.title: Overcoming Limitations in Public Cloud: Solving SUM of Rows in Custom CDS Views Using Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
In SAP Reports development, one challenge faced by developers working with CDS Views in public cloud is the inability to perform operation like SUM of rows in Custom CDS Views. 

While ‘Custom CDS Views’ application (key-user extensibility) in public cloud aims to simplify many aspects on creating a report, but some complex calculations such as summing values over multiple rows or complex calculated logical operations is not possible using ‘Custom CDS Views; app.

Problem Statement: SUM of Rows in Custom CDS Views 
In On Premise System, developers have more flexibility to create complex calculations, utilizing function like Sum(). But when working with Custom CDS Views in S/4Hana Public Cloud certain operations such as summing values across multiple rows are not supported. 
 
Solution: Using Eclipse ADT to Solve the Requirement 
Let's consider an example where multiple Material Documents (Material Docs) exist for the same material, and we need to sum up the quantities for that material.

Material Doc year 

Material DOC 

Material DOC Item 

Material 

Plant 

Quantity 

EntryUnit 

2024 

50000001 

1 

test 

1001 

10 

EA 

2024 

50000002 

1 

test 

1001 

50 

EA 

2024 

50000003 

1 

test 

1001 

5 

EA 

2024 

50000004 

1 

test 

1001 

100 

EA 

2024 

50000005 

1 

test 

1001 

65 

EA 

2024 

50000006 

1 

test 

1001 

40 

EA 

2024 

50000006 

2 

test 

1001 

25 

EA 

 
The requirement here is to sum the quantities for the same material (test) and plant (1001), regardless of the individual material document entries. 
we can solve the issue on ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse.

Steps to Implement the Solution: 
  Create a Data Definition: In Eclipse, first, create a data definition for your custom CDS view and define view. 

Here is the example code:


define view TotalQuantity
as select from I_MaterialDocumentItem_2 as a 
{

 key a.Material,  key a.Plant, 

      a.EntryUnit as Meins, 

      a.QuantityInBaseUnit, 

      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Meins' 

      sum(a.QuantityInBaseUnit) as TotalQuantity 

}

group by 

  a.Material, 

  a.Plant, 

  a.EntryUnit 

 

•  I_MaterialDocumentItem_2 is the CDS view that contains all the data related to individual Material Documents and quantity with material. 
•  The sum(a.QuantityInBaseUnit) is the operation,summing the QuantityInBaseUnit across multiple rows based on the material and plant. 
•  group by is used to group the records by Material, Plant, and EntryUnit to perform the sum. 
 
After defining save and activate

 

The Result: 

Material 

Plant 

Total Quantity 

test 

1001 

295 

@Oleg_Vokh @HakanHaslaman @krishnanunni_sudhakaranpi @Andre_Fischer @yevgen_trukhin @julieplummer20 @kaitorsten_schipper 
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Control Code /SAC/HSN Code Creation
entry.content: Dear Omprakash, you can use the tile " Mass Upload Utility" , Where you can Download the template , Edit or add HSN you wanted with descriptions and then Upload.

entry.title: Is it possible to create a CRUD SAP Fiori Elements app only using S/4 Public API?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am exploring SAP Fiori Elements and its capabilities. I am trying to create a CRUD Fiori Elements app for S/4 HANA Public Cloud Product API. I have set up a destination for the public cloud and used Fiori generator to use a List Report template. This spins out a display only Fiori elements app which is great. However, is it possible to enable Create and Delete actions just using the annotations or in any other way without making use of CAP to extend the Product API?

Many thanks!

SAP Fiori Elements SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: Re: Call transaction CJI3 with layout
entry.content: For information, the answer is program-dependent, it means that someone will look at the ABAP code for you, possibly debug for you, and answer. You might want to learn how to do it yourself. What difficulty do you have in reading ABAP code?

entry.title: Need help! How to set ETag for a PATCH request with OData V2 Remote Client Proxy
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Good Day!

Currently I'm developing a feature in SAP BTP ABAP environment, which will communicate with our SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud system through API (remote OData) to update Product Master Data.

Precisely, I'm trying to update Change Master header data through this API. I'm using OData remote client proxy with Service Consumption Model and call to a PATCH request to achieve the desired outcome.

Now the thing is, when I execute the request, it said If-Match is missing. So I try to handle this by first calling a GET request to the same Entity (with same key), then get the ETag from the response header.

After that I add the ETag as the If-Match value and call the PATCH request again. But this time the response is "If-Match is requested but no ETag property was found" and it ends up with error.

I tried multiple ways, like using HTTP client only, or change the format of the ETag value. But none of them really helps.

I'll put the source code I used below for more details.

 

 

    DATA lo_resource      TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_resource_entity.
    DATA lo_request       TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_request_update.
    DATA lo_read_request  TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_request_read.
    DATA ls_patch_req_busi_data TYPE ty_patch_request_data.
    DATA ls_read_data TYPE ty_read_data.

    IF mo_client_proxy IS INITIAL.
*      mo_client_proxy = get_client_proxy( ).
      " Create http client
      DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
                                                   comm_scenario  = '<my_communication_scenario>'
                                                   comm_system_id = '<my_communication_system>'
                                                   service_id     = '<my_service_id>' ).
      DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).

      mo_client_proxy = /iwbep/cl_cp_factory_remote=>create_v2_remote_proxy(
                            is_proxy_model_key       = VALUE #( repository_id       = 'DEFAULT'
                                                                proxy_model_id      = gc_proxy_model_id-cma
                                                                proxy_model_version = '0001' )
                            io_http_client           = lo_http_client
                            iv_relative_service_root = '' ).
    ENDIF.

    " Navigate to the resource and create a request for the update operation
    lo_resource = mo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set(
                    'A_CHANGE_MASTER' )->navigate_with_key( ms_entity_key ).

    " Get Etag
    lo_read_request = lo_resource->create_request_for_read( ).
    DATA(lo_read_response) = lo_read_request->execute( ).
    DATA(lv_etag) = lo_read_response->get_etag( ).

    lo_request = lo_resource->create_request_for_update( /iwbep/if_cp_request_update=>gcs_update_semantic-patch ).

    lo_request->set_if_match(   " <--- Error will be raised at this step
      EXPORTING
        iv_etag = lv_etag    " Not working
*        iv_etag = |W/"{ lv_etag }"|   " Not working
*        iv_etag = |W/'{ lv_etag }'|   " Not working
*        iv_etag = |W/"'{ lv_etag }'"|   " Not working
*        iv_etag = '*'                 " Not working
    ).

    ls_patch_req_busi_data = CORRESPONDING #( ms_business_data ).
    lo_request->set_business_data(
      EXPORTING
        is_business_data            = ls_patch_req_busi_data
        it_provided_property        = me->get_entity_properties( ls_patch_req_busi_data ) ).

    " Execute the request
    lo_request->execute( ).

I really needs help for this point, maybe I messed up something in between? Hopefully someone can show me the way.

Thanks a lot in advance!

entry.title: What is the need for scope items 2EL, 2EJ
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: How to setup Time Evaluation in Time Tracking
entry.content:
Hi @TB

Thanks for the great article, certainly a must for any Time Management consultant.
I've been struggling with fulfilling client requirements using yearly variables and was hoping you could provide me with some guidance:

  1. Overtime up to 100h per year should be paid according to certain premium (50%) and after 100h per year should be paid at another premium (100%)
  2. Worked holidays up to 9 a year should not have any premium paid but employee should receive one leave day from the 10th worked holiday onwards

For A. I've been able to set up a Time Container for flexible periods however I'm unable to use this value to influence the overtime calculation. While for B. I can't even set up the time container as it does not support Counter.

I would be very grateful for any guidance you may provide!
Best Regards

entry.title: I want to restrict to a fewer line items for Std odata service SD_PRICING_CONDITIONRECORD
entry.content:
Hi All,

I  want to restrict to a fewer line items for odata service SD_PRICING_CONDITIONRECORD which is used in Fiori App Manage Prices ( Sales  ) . Could you please let me know the best way for this.

1) Copy the standard Odata service into custom and create a new FIORI  app or 

2) Is  there any way that can we control the Output of standard fiori app Fiori App Manage Prices ( Sales  ).

I know we can add additional Fields using Custom Fields and Logic app .  But my question is different I want to add additional logic to the standard odata service so that I can get only records based on some condition.

 

Thanks,

Bala

entry.title: Performance Management data not available on Advanced Reporting
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to create a report on Goal Management in the Advanced Reporting tool but, for some reason, I'm not able to find the Performance Management table. nor any table related with Performance and Goals. This module is configured in the instance and we were able to find the tables on both Stories and Detailed Reporting. 

Does anyone know what could possibly be the problem?

Thank you in advance,
Pedro Ferreira

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM Validation Rules – Access additional data model tables without ABAP coding
entry.content:
Hello Sabine, first of all, thank you for sharing this blog. Your post is very informative and helpful. I’d like to share a point and get your thoughts on it.

In the blog, you noted that Process ID, Step Number, Source System, and Source ID are automatically filled. However, when I tested the same rule, these fields appeared empty for the change request process. Interestingly, when I tested it with the data quality evaluation process, these fields were filled. Do you have any idea what might be causing this discrepancy?

For Data Quality Evaluation


For Change Request

Thank you in advance for your attention. I’d like to hear your thoughts on this matter. Once again, thank you for your effort and a great blog!

entry.title: Re: System locked; license is invalid or expired
entry.content: Hi! Are you talking about the SAP license? Or the DB license? If it is the DB license, and the DB is SAP HANA, then the suggestions on the KBA mentioned on the answer that was given to this post should fix it.

entry.title: Re: Remove the share button from my SAP Fiori Element app...
entry.content: No - to my knowledge 'Send e-mail' cannot be removed when using V2.

entry.title: Turn OFF Email Notifications from SAP Business Network to Suppliers
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are enabling commerce Automation and Ariba Invoice Management with our Non-SAP ERP System. As part of this, one of our requirements is to Turn OFF all the email Notifications about Purchase Orders and Goods receipt details from SAP Business network to Supplier. Is this possible?

The Purchase Orders created in our Non-SAP ERP System today are sending email notifications to suppliers already, we don't want to confuse the suppliers with additional emails coming from SAP Business Network as it might lead to duplication of sending orders from Suppliers.

Can someone please throw some light on whether this is possible in Network and if so, what will we miss with turning OFF the notifications apart from suppliers being able to do PO flip over interactive emails over network. Please note that most of our suppliers are not over network and we would like to push the suppliers to move over to Network or have registered in network (Even if its standard account), so we can expect the invoices flowing from network to our Ariba Invoice Management for reconciliation.

Any guidance or help on how to achieve Turning OFF email notifications on PO and GR over network to suppliers would be very helpful. Also, can we turn OFF the emails in Network based on document Type, For example turning off emails for PO and GR but enabling emails for Invoices (If invoices are created from buyer)?

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Issues in extracting oracle table where security is built around it using sap DS
entry.content:
We are working on a project migrating oracle ERP data to SAP HANA Using SAP data services. Our Current SAP HANA Version is HANA 2.0 SPS 05.   SAP Data Services  4.3 SPS 02

There are certain tables in Oracle where security is build around it.

We have a run a procedure before viewing the data in Oracle .  MO_GLOBAL.SET_POLICY_CONTEXT ('S','ORG_ID')  and run the select query to read the data.

These should happen in the same session, but when trying to do this in SAP Data services, the session changes.  

Please provide your suggestions on extracting data from security built tables.

Regards,

Karthik Rajan P

entry.title: Rounding off difference by vendor in MIRO
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Our client request is to have the document total rounding off by vendor wise.

 Example: Vendor invoice 10100.00 INR whereas our MIRO will be created 10100.49 INR further we will also will get the vendor payment for 10100 only INR. so in this case in F-53 the difference amount has been taking ZDI transaction key. 

Now we decided our clients requirement is to be rounding off the MIRO document total as 10100.00INR as like vendor invoice total.

Note:  Only for few vendor invoices is required in our company code.

Our Analysis is that we have tested and checked this company level possibility via OMR6 and OMRX.

but we are expecting vendor wise rounding off.

I have created Tolerance key and assigned in the vendor master level also, this process also not helpful of my requirement.

Thanks & Regards,

Saikrishna

entry.title: Re: ENTRY COMPANY CODE IS MISSING IN TABLE T043G
entry.content: TO43g

Hi All,

For calculating Depreciation Shift Wise in some of the assets currently we are manually posting through Unplanned Depreciation.

But we want from SAP only it should calculate for which we are maintaining Shift Factor as per the requirement i,e 1, 1.5 and 2 along with Variable Depreciation Portion in Asset Master in change mode for which we are receiving error. Request to please help us out. FYI we have V3 Fiscal Year Variant.

 

 

 

 

All the configuration has been check and proper . For some of the Assets no issue. Asset Transfer Date 30.09.2017

SAP Community FIN (Finance) FIN Asset Accounting

entry.title: How to extend the Delivery note form to enable commodity code in output?
entry.content:
1. Do we need to extend the Delivery note form to enable commodity code in output?
  2. Is there any config we need to do so that it comes to delivery note output?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to extend the Delivery note form to enable commod...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

You need to extend the Delivery Note form to enable the commodity code in the output. This can be probably done by adding custom fields to the form template. Here are the steps to do this:

  1. Create the required custom fields in the Custom Fields app. You can assign the business context ‘Shipping: Delivery’ or ‘Shipping: Delivery Item’ depending on where you want the commodity code to appear.
  2. Enable the usage of the custom fields for the target form template and publish them. This will make the custom fields available for use in the form template.
  3. Add logic to your new custom fields. This can be done manually or by implementing Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) in the Custom Logic app.
  4. Define your own PDF form for output in the Maintain Form Templates app. You do this by downloading and editing the standard form template file (XDP) with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. After that, you upload the PDF form as a custom template in the original language.
  5. After these steps, the commodity code should be visible in the Delivery Note output.

For further information, please check the documents below:

Output Extensibility

Output Management

entry.title: READ TABLE WITH DYNAMIC VARIABLE
entry.content:
Hello experts on line,

following is my code.

TYPES: BEGIN OF t_str,
  col1 TYPE c,
  col2 TYPE i,
  END OF t_str.


TYPES: t_itab type TABLE of t_str.
data: gs_str type t_str,
      gt_itab type t_itab.

data gv_name type char10 value 'COL1'.

gs_str-col1 = 'A'.
gs_str-col2 = 1.
APPEND gs_str to gt_itab.

gs_str-col1 = 'B'.
gs_str-col2 = 2.
APPEND gs_str to gt_itab.

PERFORM test_itab USING gt_itab.

*FORM test_itab using pt_itab type t_itab.
*  READ TABLE pt_itab with key col1 = 'A' into gs_str.
*  if sy-subrc = 0.
*    write: gs_str-col1, gs_str-col2.
*  ENDIF.
*endform.

FORM test_itab using pt_itab type ANY TABLE.
  READ TABLE pt_itab with key (gv_name) = 'A' into gs_str.
  if sy-subrc = 0.
    write: gs_str-col1, gs_str-col2.
  ENDIF.
endform.

I don't know why it does works with dynamic variable (gv_name) when I choose 'type any table'.

Could you please do me a favor to explain a little bit? Thanks

entry.title: Re: ME55 release option rejection SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

In after execute ME55 > Goto > Individual Release

Now Select any PR, now can see reject button on PR.

Regards

entry.title: SAP MM
Billing relevance D in execution order items in Service with Advanced Execution
entry.content:
The concept of Billing Relevance in Service with Advanced Execution is already explained in a previous blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/billing-relevance-in-execution-order-item/ba-p/13574529

This blog is to showcase the Service with Advanced execution process flow with a specific type of billing relevance which is Billing on Completion (Key = D).

In SAP S/4HANA Service, "Billing on Completion" means that the billing process can start only once the service execution is completed, this means, when the status is set to “Completed”.  Typically, this process is started with the creation of a billing document request (BDR).

The quantity defined in a transaction, or the fixed price agreed for a service is billed once the transaction is completed.

The following steps depicts the process flow for Billing on Completion:

1. Create a service order and add an execution order item.

Service Order with an execution order item

2. Navigate to the item overview page in Edit mode, scroll down to the Billing assignment block and change Billing Relevance to Billing on Completion.

Billing Assignment block in execution order item

3. Release the item and save the service order.

Release execution order item

Maintenance order is generated, and the execution status of execution order item is set to Execution Planning.

Execution status after creation of maintenance order

4. Release the maintenance order after planning.

Releasing the maintenance order

5. Set the status of maintenance order to Technically Completed after the execution is complete.

Setting status to Technically Completed

This sets the execution order item status to Completed and execution status to Execution Done.

Execution status after technically completing the maintenance order

6. Open the Release for Billing app. Enter the service order ID and search. From the results, select the service order ID and then choose Release for Billing.

Once you choose Release for Billing, the service order disappears from the search results. In background, a Billing Document Request (BDR) is created corresponding to the service order item.

Release for Billing app

Back in the service order, the Transfer to Billing status is set to Successful. Also, the BDR number will be visible in Transaction History.

Billing assignment block

7. Open the Create Billing Documents app and search using the service order ID. Select the order from the search result and choose Create Billing Documents.

Create Billing Documents app

The BDRs are processed to create a customer invoice containing the details of the completed service. 

Invoice

The Invoice (Billing Document) is also updated in the transaction history of the service order and the Billing Status is set to Completely billed.              

Billing and Transaction History of execution order item after billing

8. Lastly, you can post the Billing Document to complete the process.

Post Billing Document

With this, we can understand how Billing on Completion works in service.

entry.title: Unleashing the Power of LLMs with SAP HANA Cloud as Vector DB on SAP BTP: A Python-Powered RAG App
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's data-driven world, businesses are increasingly turning to Large Language Models (LLMs) to unlock valuable insights from their vast troves of information. This blog post explores how to leverage the power of LLMs in conjunction with SAP HANA Cloud as Vector DB on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to efficiently store, retrieve, and analyze textual data. We will delve into the core concepts of embedding generation, vector databases, and demonstrate a practical Python application showcasing these RAG capabilities.

Understanding Embeddings

At the heart of this solution lies the concept of embeddings. Embeddings are numerical representations of text, capturing the semantic meaning and relationships between words and phrases. By transforming text into dense vectors, we enable machines to understand and process language in a more meaningful way. LLMs, such as those offered by OpenAI, excel at generating high-quality embeddings that accurately reflect the underlying semantics of the text.

The Role of SAP HANA Cloud as Vector DB

While LLMs provide the foundation for semantic understanding, efficient storage and retrieval of these embeddings are crucial. This is where SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB comes into play. This powerful database leverages advanced indexing techniques specifically designed for vector data, enabling lightning-fast similarity searches. By storing document embeddings within HANA Vector DB, we can efficiently retrieve the most relevant information based on semantic similarity, rather than relying solely on keyword matching.

Leveraging the LangChain Framework

To streamline the development process, we will utilize the LangChain framework. LangChain provides a comprehensive suite of tools and utilities for building and interacting with LLMs, including components for:

  • Embedding Generation: Integrating with popular LLM providers like OpenAI to generate embeddings for your text data.
  • Vector Database Integration: Seamlessly connecting with HANA Vector DB to store and retrieve embeddings.
  • Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): Combining the power of LLMs with external knowledge sources (like HANA Vector DB) to enhance the quality and relevance of LLM outputs.

 Tech Stack Architecture

Building a Python Application

Create an Application with Cloud Foundry Python Buildpack

Pre-requisites: Create SAP HANA instance in BTP (I used trial account) and create a Dev space

Tools : VSCode

Python:3.12

Libraries : langchain,hdbcli,langchain-community,pypdf2,openai

Embedding : openai embedding

Let's outline the key steps involved in building a Python application that leverages these technologies:

Step 1 : Create python application

Section 1.1: Loading the PDF and Creating Chunks

def process_pdf(file_path):
  # Read PDF
  reader = PdfReader(file_path)
  text = ""
  for page in reader.pages:
    text += page.extract_text()
  # Split text into chunks
  text_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(
      chunk_size=500,
      chunk_overlap=50,
      length_function=len,
      is_separator_regex=False
  )
  chunks = text_splitter.split_text(text)
  return chunks

This section focuses on preparing the textual data for further processing.

  • process_pdf function: This function takes the file path of a PDF document as input.
  • Read PDF: It uses the PdfReader class (likely from an external library) to read the content of the PDF.
  • Extract Text: The code iterates through each page of the PDF and extracts the text using the extract_text() method. All the extracted text is concatenated into a single string.
  • Splitting into Chunks: Here, a RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter class (likely from LangChain) is used to split the extracted text into smaller chunks. This is done for two reasons:
  • LLM Processing Limits: LLMs often have limitations on the amount of text they can process at once. Splitting into chunks ensures the text stays within these limits.
  • Improved Efficiency: Processing smaller chunks can be more efficient for both the LLM and the overall application.

The function returns a list of text chunks after processing the PDF.

Section 1.2: Creating HANA DB Connection

def store_in_hana(chunks, filename):
  # Initialize HANA connection
  HANA_CONNECTION = dbapi.connect(
      address="xxxx.hana.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com",
      port="443",
      user=os.environ.get('HANA_USER'),
      password=os.environ.get('HANA_PASSWORD'),
      autocommit=True,
      sslValidateCertificate=False
  )

This section establishes a connection to the SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB instance.

  • store_in_hana function: This function takes the list of text chunks and the original filename of the PDF as input.
  • HANA Connection: It uses the dbapi.connect function to establish a connection with the HANA database. The connection details like address, port, username, and password are provided (likely retrieved from environment variables for security).

Note: Replace the placeholder values with your actual HANA Cloud connection details.

Section 1.3: Embedding and Storing in HANA Cloud Vector DB

  # Initialize embeddings model
  embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()
  # Initialize vector store
  vectorstore = HanaDB(
      connection=HANA_CONNECTION,
      embedding=embeddings,
      table_name="UPLOAD_DOCUMENT_VECTORS"
  )
  # Create documents with metadata
  texts = chunks
  metadatas = [{"source": filename, "chunk": i} for i in range(len(chunks))]
  # Add documents to vector store
  vectorstore.add_texts(texts=texts, metadatas=metadatas)
  return len(chunks)

This section leverages LangChain and HANA Vector DB to process the text chunks and store them efficiently.

  • Embedding Model: An OpenAIEmbeddings instance is created (likely from LangChain) to interact with an OpenAI LLM for generating embeddings.
  • Vector Store: A HanaDB instance is created, specifying the HANA connection, the embedding model, and the target table name ("UPLOAD_DOCUMENT_VECTORS") within the HANA database. This essentially creates a bridge between LangChain and HANA Vector DB.
  • Documents and Metadata: The texts variable holds the list of text chunks.The metadatas list creates metadata entries for each chunk, including the source filename (original PDF) and a chunk identifier (index within the list).
  • Adding Documents: The add_texts method of the vectorstore object is used to add the text chunks and their corresponding metadata

 Section 1.4: Retrieval and LLM Interaction

  • Create a ConversationalRetrievalChain using LangChain to facilitate interaction with the LLM.
  • When a user query is received, generate an embedding for the query.
  • Use the HANA Vector DB to retrieve the most similar documents based on the query embedding.
  • Pass the retrieved documents to the LLM along with the user query.
  • The LLM will then generate a response based on both the user query and the relevant context from the retrieved documents.

Section 1.5: Create a HTML page for the UI

  • Create a ConversationalRetrievalChain using LangChain to facilitate interaction with the LLM.
  • When a user query is received, generate an embedding for the query.
  • Use the HANA Vector DB to retrieve the most similar documents based on the query embedding.
  • Pass the retrieved documents to the LLM along with the user query.
  • The LLM will then generate a response based on both the user query and the relevant context from the retrieved documents.

Section 1.6: Test the app locally in VS code 

  • Run the .py file to check if the application is working locally.

        Open the URL in browser locally

Step 2 : Push the application to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP

Install the cloudfoundry-cli as per the installation instructions mentioned below .

https://github.com/cloudfoundry/cli/wiki/V8-CLI-Installation-Guide

choco install cloudfoundry-cli

Check if you can access the SAP HANA Cloud DB endpoint api using the below command

cf api https://api.cf.xxxxx.hana.ondemand.com

Login using cf login –sso to authenticate. It will create a temporary link , use that link to login and get the temporary token and use it to authenticate

In the VS code terminal go to the app folder and then use the below command to push the app to the BTP

Cf push

The app will be visible in the BTP DEV space

Launch the app by clicking on the Application Routes URL as shown below

Test the File upload application

I have used the “Installation Guide for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023” pdf for this.

Test the RAG application by running retriever app .

The source code is available in the git repo here  SAP-HANA-VectorDB-RAG 

Conclusion

By combining the power of LLMs, SAP HANA Vector DB, and the LangChain framework, you can build intelligent applications that can effectively understand, process, and utilize your textual data. This approach opens up a wide range of possibilities, including:

  • Enhanced Customer Support: Providing more accurate and personalized support through chatbots and virtual assistants.
  • Improved Search Capabilities: Enabling users to find relevant information more efficiently through semantic search.
  • Intelligent Document Analysis: Extracting key insights and automating tasks such as document summarization and classification.

This blog post has provided a high-level overview of this exciting technology. By exploring and experimenting further, you can unlock the full potential of LLMs and HANA Vector DB to drive innovation and transform your business processes.

References :

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-cf-buildpacks-python-create.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/a-journey-into-retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-on-sap-btp/ba-p/13580653

entry.title: Re: Setup of the SAP Signavio Process Insights connector within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

Thanks for the detailed information. 

the data load and flow process:

from ERP --> Signavio Process insights--> Signavio Process Intelligence.

when we extract the data from ERP, will the data reside in the tables of Signavio Process insights or virtual?

when we extract the data from Signavio Process Insights to Process intelligence, is that physical extraction or virtual?

in this whole scenario, what is the role of the BTP?

Thanks

Reddy

entry.title: Re: Doubt for optimize abap code
entry.content:
On a Hana system, you can inner join the ITAB with the database table directly; without FOR ALL ENTRIES.

select  
  from is_ee_org_ass_repl_req-org_assignment as _oa
       inner join hrp1001 
               on hrp1001~plvar = '01' and
                  hrp1001~subty = 'A012' and
                  hrp1001~objid = _oa~position_id and
                  hrp1001~begda <= _oa~start_date and
                  hrp1001~endda >= _oa~end_date
  fields _oa~position_id,hrp1001~sobid
  into table @DATA(results).

Dan van Leeuwen

entry.title: S4HANA Variant Configuration SD Settings
entry.content:
Hello All, 

I would like to know Variant Configuration SD Functional settings and Integration settings.  Hope we can use Variant Configuration MTS and MTO.

 Make to stockMake to order
General Item category group00020002
Material Master -  Item category group

ERLA or 0002

LUMF or 0004

Sale order item category determination VOV4ERLA or 0002

LUMF or 0004

Delivery item category determination 0184ERLA or 0002

LUMF or 0004

Pricing at Header Item category VOV7Don't knowDon't know
Pricing at Line item Item category VOV7Don't knowDon't know
User of Class type300 or 200300 or 200
Strategy group 25 or 2625 or 26

Which Functional consultant is responsible for below 

Create characteristics T-Code CT04

Creation of dependency source code

Create Class T-Code CL01

Create configuration profile CU41

Variant Tables & Variant Function

Dependency nets T-Code CU21

Routing T-Code CA01

Create dependency T-Code CU01

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAC Live models slow performance in story

performance problems can have many causes (but they all can be solved).

Also, in this book there are many tips to address performance problems
https://www.sap-press.com/sap-analytics-cloud-performance-optimization-guide_5669/

regards

entry.title: Time Management Exceptional Scenarios – A Guidebook
entry.content:
To address these issues, we have devised some solutions that I would like to share with the community to help tackle such problems faced by clients.

 

Problem Statement 1: Accrual Run for Next Year for exceptional cases before actual run date

The accrual run date for the next year is set for December 15, 2024. However, some employees want to apply for leaves in advance, which can be random from any division or department. To manage this, we will run accruals from Manage Time off Calendars. Since this will give accruals to all employees, we need a filtering criterion that is not available in Manage Time off Calendars. Therefore, we will tweak the accrual rule and introduce a flag. If the flag needs to be associated with an event or event reason, introduce it in Job Information. If it needs to be independent, create a Custom MDF and introduce the flag there. Here are the steps:

  1. Create a custom MDF with the flag.

  1. Call this flag in accrual rule

     2. Upload the flag values for the employees you want to run the accrual rule for. The rule will work for all employees on or after December 15 if the Time off calendar job is run. If the accrual is run before December 15, it will only apply to employees marked with a "Y" flag for enabling leave quota generation.

      3. Run Manage Time off calendars – Accrual job for all the employees.

 

Note 1: You can apply the same method to stop accruals for some employees at the end of the year due to client-specific reasons.

Note 2: We must run accrual once for all the employees else recalculation will not happen for the employees for whom “Accrual Period ID” is missing which can be generated by running accruals only from Manage time off calendars

Problem Statement 2: Extending the Bookable End Date

To extend the bookable end date for a few employees, you can:

  1. Import the Time accounts for the users you want to update the bookable end date for:
    • Export the Time accounts from “Import and Export Data.”
    • Update the bookable end date for the respective users and import the file back.
  2. Update the Time account manually if the users are few:
    • Go to Manage Time off structure, search for the Time account external code or User ID you want to update, click on Take Action, make the correction, update the bookable end date, and click on Save.

 

Additional Client Questions:

1. How can we close some accounts but keep others open at the end of the year?

  • Answer: Run PEP for all accounts, then set the flag for “Closed to No” either by import or by updating individual time accounts under Manage Time off Structures, as mentioned in Problem Statement 2.

 

 

2. How can we carry forward balances prior to running PEP for the year when the bookable end date is later than the validity date?

Answer:

  • First, write an interim rule to post the carry forward balance to the time account by adding a custom field for balance storage. This rule can be run multiple times to simulate the carry forward balance.
  • Attach this rule to the Time account type for which carry forward needs to be done.

Below is the snapshot of the rule for reference:

 

 

 

  • Second, write an interim rule in the time account where the carry forward needs to be posted, calling the value from balance storage. This rule should be run only once.

3. Post carryforward, adjustment of leaves required as during the carryforward period some employees might have utilized some leaves if black out period is not allowed in organization.

 

Answer: If the Numbers are handful, adjustments can be done from manual adjustments for each employee using Time administration UI, but if Numbers are more then we can upload Time account Details with Manual adjustment.

I will keep on adding the new scenarios to this blog if I come across any, hopefully this blog will help the members of the community to find better solutions for the clients.

entry.title: Harnessing Custom Notifications for Optimizing Single-User POD Utilization
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Digital Manufacturing, notifications play a crucial role in various scenarios in the background. Whether it involves creating alerts, displaying messages on POD screens, or facilitating communication between different POD pages, this underlying structure is highly beneficial. By adapting notifications within Core Plugin Extensions, a versatile solution is created, allowing for custom implementations and scenarios, and providing a robust framework to address diverse operational needs.

This article is concise and underscores the importance and flexibility of notifications in SAP Digital Manufacturing, clearly illustrating their application in various contexts.

In the following sections, we will explain several cases that can be addressed through notifications. One scenario will demonstrate how a selected POD can be configured to allow only one user to work with an SFC at a time, preventing users from overwriting each other’s data on the same POD screen. Another scenario will show how a message can be sent to a POD specific to a user, as messages sent via the POD message plugin in the Design Production Processes App are currently broadcast to all users who match the subscription settings. This blog post will also cover the solution to this issue.

Notifications in DM

Whether we realize it or not, the standard notifications feature operates actively in the background of standard DM POD screens. An example of this can be seen when sending a POD Message through Process Designer, where subscription information must be filled out.

["Figure 1 - Pod Message Warning"]

With this method, DM ensures the desired communication by sending the triggered notification type and input to every user that matches the given subscription parameters.

["Figure 2 - Pod Message with Message Notification"]

The capabilities of the existing notification structure were enhanced through the implementation of the Notification Send API. This API enabled the realization of various triggering scenarios, significantly broadening the system's functionality.

With the Notification Send API, additional developments can be made, such as enabling communication between pages, real-time UI changes on pages used by other users, and displaying instant screen messages and warning messages in PODs.

POD Logic for the Single-User Based Scenario

The operator selects the order they will be working on and is subsequently directed to the labor tracking page, where they are expected to initiate the labor time tracking process by using the Labor-On button. By integrating an extension within a plugin located in the labor tracking page, a process is triggered upon pressing the Labor-On button. The operator can't proceed to the other page without using the button. This button triggers our labor-on process thanks to the related extension, which, in turn, calls the Find All Time Records service with the Operation, Plant, and SFC information to check if any user is already tracking labor for the selected SFC under the DIRECT_LABOR type.

If the service returns a null result, indicating that no active labor is being tracked for the SFC, the Direct Labor Start process is initiated, and the user will be navigated to the details page, where they can be execute operations.

If the service returns a user ID who is the same as the one clicks on the Labor-On button, the process output ‘LaborExists’ is sent as empty data, and the user will be navigated to the details page.

However, if the returned user ID is different from the one trying to track labor on the SFC the process first triggers the 'Create Stop Event For Direct Labor On An SFC' service with the returned user’s ID, and then initiates the 'Create Start Event For Labor On An SFC' event for the user who triggered the button. The ID of the user whose labor tracking is stopped is then sent as the process output data.

["Figure 3 - Labor On Process"]

 

 

Based on the result of this process triggered within the extension, if the returned ‘LaborExists’ data is neither empty nor null, a custom notification that we created within the Notification Extension is triggered along with the returned user ID.

When the custom notification is triggered, everyone using the same POD and subscribed to the same custom notification within the extension will receive this notification. Upon receiving the notification, a function we wrote is triggered to handle it by comparing the page user's ID and the ID sent as the process output data.

If the user ID in the process output data matches with the user who received the notification, the user is navigated to the Main Page from the Details page and a message box indicating the labor tracking event for the user is stopped will appear on the screen of the navigated user.

["Figure 4 - Incoming Custom Notification"]

As a result, the previous user tracking the labor on an SFC, whose labor has been off due to the new user labor on event, will need to trigger the labor on process again to continue. This ensures that the operations performed for an SFC through this POD, are effectively managed as a Single-User Utilization through the extension.

Future Directions

As we continue to refine and optimize the use of notifications in SAP Digital Manufacturing PODs, several potential enhancements could further elevate the user experience and system efficiency. Expanding the custom notification framework to support more complex, multi-user scenarios could provide greater flexibility in managing shared resources. These future enhancements hold the potential to make SAP Digital Manufacturing PODs even more robust, versatile, and user specific, ensuring they continue to meet the demands of modern manufacturing environments.

A significant enhancement could involve delivering targeted alerts to users subscribed to specific notifications within the POD, displaying custom messages on their screens, sending real-time notifications via Microsoft Teams, and even triggering email alerts. These improvements would greatly enhance the system's responsiveness and interactivity.

 Demo Video

If you like this post or have any questions, please let us know in the comments.
All questions are highly appreciated.

Dilahan Çiloğlu & Ege Aksöyek

entry.title: Condition record exists, but has not been set
entry.content:
Hello,

When i try to create a STO (me21n), in the condition type ZDRR it is displaying "condition record exists but has not been set" in the condition types analysis section. if i change the valid from 01/01/2025 to 01/01/2024 it is working fine means it is displaying "condition record exists". if i change valid from to current date (26/12/2024) then again it is displaying "condition record exists but has not been set". Please let me know your valuable suggestions.

entry.title: FIELD TO CLASIFY ORDER REQUEST (SOLPED)
entry.content:
Hi experts

The purchasing area requires that the purchase requisition includes a classification that makes it possible to identify whether it is a regularization, an emergency purchase or a normal purchase.

For this purpose, it is necessary to enable or use a standard field in SAP that meets the following characteristics:

Reportable in ME5A.
Predefined value list. This will avoid manual typing and reduce possible errors.
Mandatory field.

After exploring the available fields in the requisition, I identified that the “Requirement No.” field could be used for this purpose. However, this field does not meet all the necessary requirements. I have also considered the option of creating a new requisition type.

I would appreciate your guidance, based on your experience, to address this requirement in the most appropriate manner.

Thanks, Percy.

Hi,

I'm getting the similar error; did you find out what the problem was here?
It'd be really helpful if you could share. 

When I give the details of the system for Basic connection type, I'm getting an error "Pre-requisites are missing".

entry.title: What is practical skill test?
entry.content:
I have scheduled my SAP Certification exam for C_TS4FI_2023 and in appoinment mail there is one special instruction as mentioned below-

For this exam - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting - the candidate must share the screen showing proof of completing practical skills test.

Can anyone help me with this? From where and how can I take this practical skill test?

entry.title: Re: Request Header Is To large
entry.content:
In %TomcatDir%\Conf is a file called server.xml.  For the connector ports, (80, 443, or whichever you use) there is a parameter that is added to support Single Sign-on called maxHttpHeaderSize.

If you don't have this parameter for your connector port(s), you can add it.  The setting used for Single Sign-on is maxHttpHeaderSize="65536" added at the end of the line.

If you already have this parameter, try increasing it.

I'm not sure if this parameter is set anywhere else besides this.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Error Message Display in RAP Managed Scena...
entry.content: Are you using this base class CL_ABAP_BEHAVIOR_SAVER_FAILED when you implemented the unmanaged save? Or just the CL_ABAP_BEHAVIOR_SAVER class?

entry.title: Translation of SAP FIORI Using SE63
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP's SE63 tool enables the translation of various text content within SAP systems, providing centralized translation management essential for international operations. Translating Fiori applications is crucial as it allows users to work in their own language, improving user experience and productivity. Through SE63, elements like fields, buttons, and messages in Fiori applications can be managed in multiple languages. This process ensures a consistent and cohesive user interface across different languages, offering a robust experience for global users.

In this blog, I will walk you through how to leverage SE63 for translating SAP Fiori tiles.

Let's Start

Please follow the steps below.

Step 1:Run the SE63 transaction code through SAP GUI to access the translation tool.

Choose the "Short Texts" button.

Choose > "00 Meta Objects" > select "TABL Tables (Meta)".

Enter the object name *WDY_CONF_USERT2*.

Enter the source and target languages and select "Edit".

Enter the "User Scope" to "A"

Enter the ID of your catalog you created in the Configuration ID field or you can use the search help on Configuration ID to find your catalog.

Step 2:Enter translations for the texts you want to translate and save.

Step 3:Clear cache for changes to be reflected in launchpad.

To clear the cache, go to transaction "SE38" > enter "/UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES" > run in execution mode.

Result

You can now display your custom tiles, catalogs or group titles along with the intended text in the SAP Fiori Launchpad.

entry.title: Use Azure AD groups as recipient groups in SBPA
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

 

is it possible to use Azure AD groups as recipient groups in SBPA process approval user task while specifying recipient's , where IAS will act as proxy and delegate authentication from corporate IDP and IAS user store is disabled, will this setup works ,where Azure Ad group can be directly specified in the SBPA approval user task ?

Thanks,
Siddharth

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Update the amount for a condition type at delivery level

I think , you can achieve this requirement by using the LE_SHP_DELIVERY_PROC BADI or EXIT_SAPLV50E_002. 

Kindly verify

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am getting error "CSRF Token Validation Failed" in POST request API of my SAP system since I'm generating CSRF token using GET request and pass the same to POST request in same SAP testing client.

I am able to generate CSRF token successfully through below code using get_header_field  thru GET request. Also, I' m passing the same token to get_header_field  thru  POST request with my successful authendication. However,  getting an error as "CSRF Token Validation failed"

Please advise where I am doing wrong. Also, I am able to post successfully via POSTMAN with same data and Basic Authentication using POSTMAN GET and POST request in the same testing client.

My Code:

lv_url = |http://MyURL|.

    " Create HTTP client instance
    CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
      EXPORTING
        url                = lv_url
      IMPORTING
        client             = lo_http_client.

* create the URI for the client.

  l_query = lv_url.

  CALL METHOD cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri

    EXPORTING

      request = lo_http_client->request

      uri     = l_query.

* update the HTTP Method

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_method

    EXPORTING

      method = lo_http_client->request->co_request_method_get.

*set Content type

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->if_http_entity~set_content_type

    EXPORTING

      content_type =  'application/json'.

lo_http_client->authenticate(   username = 'username' "iv_username'
                                password = 'password'
                                client   = '900'
                                language = 'E' ).
* set header field for fetching X-CSRF token

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_header_field

    EXPORTING

      name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

      value = 'Fetch'.

 lo_http_client->send(

      EXCEPTIONS

        http_communication_failure = 1

        http_invalid_state         = 2 ). "Send the HTTP request

  lo_http_client->receive(

    EXCEPTIONS

      http_communication_failure = 1

      http_invalid_state         = 2

      http_processing_failed     = 3 ). "receive the response

****GET x-csrf TOKEN from earlier response

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->response->get_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

  RECEIVING

    value = l_token.

data : lv_http_status type i,
       lv_status_text type string.
    " Check for successful response
  lo_http_client->response->get_status( IMPORTING
     CODE   =     lv_http_status             " HTTP Status Code
     REASON =     lv_status_text             " HTTP status description
).
* Set X-CSRF- Token in the new request.

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

    value = l_token.

*lo_http_client->set_csrf_token( l_token ) ,  .

* update the HTTP Method

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_method

  EXPORTING

    method = lo_http_client->request->co_request_method_post.

****content type

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_content_type

  EXPORTING

    content_type = 'application/json'.

l_body = '{"MANDT":"900","ID":"0000000002","CUST_NAME":"POST_METHOD","INV_NO":"1234567890"}'.

lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( DATA = l_body ).

* create Body for the HTTP Post request

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_cdata

  EXPORTING

    data = l_body.

lo_http_client->send(

    EXCEPTIONS

      http_communication_failure = 1

      http_invalid_state         = 2 ). "Send the HTTP request

lo_http_client->receive(

  EXCEPTIONS

    http_communication_failure = 1

    http_invalid_state         = 2

    http_processing_failed     = 3 ). "receive the response

l_result = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

WRITE : / L_RESULT.

entry.title: Categorize Time Valuation Counter in 3 groups using time collector
entry.content:
Is there a way to use time valuations to categorize the times an employee is late? For example, if an employee is late 10 times in a month, we would like to split it into 3 time type groups their different penalty in payroll:
  • The first late occurrence equals 1.
  • The second late occurrence equals 1.
  • The third group includes the remaining 8 late occurrences.
Looking forward to your advice on this implementation.

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certificatio...
entry.content: @Ajinkya1 How do you know whether it's a Web site issue or a change in policy? Did you contact SAP by "creating a case"?

entry.title: Re: CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content: I guess you should not reuse the same client object for several requests. Create a new one for each request you want to send (e.g. "cl_http_client=>create_by_url").

entry.title: CNDJ Submission Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to submit the Cloud Native Developer Journey Bulletinboard-ads-nodejs exercise but for some reason, I'm not getting an email at all.  Previously when I submitted it, I I received an email telling me that it had errors that I had to correct, which I did.  

Now, I'm getting nothing.

Any ideas and/or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Ron

entry.title: Re: Product Compliance - Tables used for REACH SVHC
entry.content:
Hello

we need to pay attention regarding the answer to your question. 

We have now two products (of SAP) in place which are used to manage the information you are looking for.

One is "SAP EHS Classic" and the new solution is called "SAP Product Compliance",

The topic of SVHC and "SAP EHS Classic" is discussed yet here. I am not aware of any discussion regarding the new soluton (SAP Product Compliance).

Anyhow: in EHS sector it is not a good option to look on "table" level ( i skip the reason for this).

E.g. you will find some threads regarding your topic

Example is: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/article-and-or-product-compostion-for-reach-svhc-volume-tracking-in-ehsm/qaq-p/723867

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/substance-volume-tracking-using-sap-ehs-management/ba-p/13272665 can may be help as well.

Your question (or at least a part of it) was discussed e.g. here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/product-compliance-table-used-for-rohs-reach-weee-and-other-regulatory-list/qaq-p/12601308

As long as we do not know which current SAP solution you are using: it is not easy to help you.

In most of the cases: it is better to check for an "API/BAPI" to look for the data. For EHS Classic: this is more or less a "must" (because of severla reasons)

Wish you the best

C.B

PS: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/138-svhc-substances-are-your-products-impacted/ba-p/12992898

might be of interest

Definition of the term:

A substance of very high concern (SVHC) is a chemical substance (or part of a group of chemical substances) which has been proposed as a candidate for inclusion on the Authorization or Restriction list (see: ECHA Lists) of REACH.

entry.title: Re: DataAction copy step
entry.content: Right now i have my data actions like this and its working, but its not dynamic, i would like to make it dynamic

entry.title: Re: 10 Steps to Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Customers
entry.content:

I just onboarded in the role as EA-Rise in the region and found the info and this bootcamp could be pretty useful for a bunch of customers I am engaging with, among which some of them are currently planning for RISE. I am wondering if this offer is still valid in 2025, and I would be interested to promote this as well as Clean Core in the region. 

Thanks.

BR, Stan

entry.title: How to make a freight order ready for posting without WM module?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are configuring S4 HANA Grow with SAP Public Cloud for demo purposes without implementing the WM module.

How we can make a freight order ready for posting without WM module?

We are requesting confirmation if WM module implementation is mandatory or if there is an alternative solution.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to make a freight order ready for posting without...
entry.content:
To make a freight order ready for posting without the Warehouse Management (WM) module, you need to follow these steps:

  1. Create Freight Units: When you create or edit sales orders, the system automatically creates freight units considering freight unit building rules. You can view these freight units in the ‘Manage Freight Units’ app or in the transportation cockpit.

  2. Create a Freight Document: You can create a freight document based on freight units using the ‘Transportation Cockpit’ app. Alternatively, you can create a freight document using the list view of the apps in the Freight Order Management area. You can then assign freight units to these freight documents.

  3. Edit and Enhance the Freight Document: Open the ‘Manage Freight Orders’ app, search for your freight document and select it for editing in the detailed view of the app. 

  4. Perform Charge Calculation: When you save your freight document, the system calculates the charges automatically. However, you can also trigger charge calculation manually from the list view or detailed view of the ‘Manage Freight Orders’ app.

  5. Set the Ready for Transportation Execution status: Set the ‘Ready for Transportation Execution’ status on the header level of the freight document.

  6. Perform Subcontracting and Send the Freight Document to the Carrier: You can also send shipping notifications to the carrier. The communication is performed via EDI messages.

  7. Create Deliveries for the Freight Document: You can create deliveries based on the freight orders.

  8. Track the Execution of the Freight Order: Open the ‘Monitor Execution - Freight Orders’ app from the launchpad. When the truck has arrived at your location, set the handling execution status for your transportation stage and source location to ‘Arrived’ using the ‘Execution Status (Source)’ dropdown menu. When the papers of the truck have been checked successfully, set the handling execution status to ‘Checked In’. When the goods have been loaded, set the handling execution status to ‘Checked Out’. 

  9. Post Accruals for Transportation Costs: Using the ‘Monitor Accrual Postings - Freight Orders’ app, you can confirm the freight orders that are ready for accrual posting to Financial Accounting

For further information, please check:

Manage Freight Orders

Transportation Management ™ - Monitor Execution - Freight Orders

Transportation Planning - Sample Outbound Process

entry.title: How to Track Payment Term Changes in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Does anyone have a way to track changes made in S/4 HANA to payment terms on all customers? A Report or a Fiori app that can track changes on all customers?

SAP S/4HANA FIN (Finance) SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

entry.title: Abap rap unmanaged with custom buttons only.
entry.content:
Dear friends,

In Abap RAP Fiori list reporting using custom buttons for posting line items and

update results to lineitems of screen,

Response popup message.

Note: no drafting/ CUD

In BD implement:

Unmanaged zbp_xyz as unquie:

{  fields { read only } field1,field2,-----field n;

Action {feature; instance }<action> results;

//Action <name> results;

}

1. Unable to use modify entities in custom action method to update results to column fields (⚠️ message, dump as illegal statement).

2. Suggest how achieve with side effects to refresh child entity screen with parent.

entry.title: Multiple work item execution together not possible in SBWP
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We are trying to execute multiple work items in a single run. All the items are in the same group and the work item is dialog work items.

 

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Multiple work item execution together not possible in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Your scenario and the solution is addressed by KBA 2136205 - SBWP : Mass Execution of work items.

 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Save the Date for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content: when are the release dates?

entry.title: Re: CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content:
Thanks Sandra...

I followed your advise,

Please check below code, however I'm getting same error as CSRF token validation failed

Also, I added Cookies in both GET and POST request

Please help me where I'm wrong

 

 

lv_url = |http://MyURL|.

    " Create HTTP client instance
    CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
      EXPORTING
        url                = lv_url
      IMPORTING
        client             = lo_http_client.

* create the URI for the client.

  l_query = lv_url.

  CALL METHOD cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri

    EXPORTING

      request = lo_http_client->request

      uri     = l_query.

* update the HTTP Method

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_method

    EXPORTING

      method = lo_http_client->request->co_request_method_get.

* set Content type

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->if_http_entity~set_content_type

    EXPORTING

      content_type =  'application/json'.

lo_http_client->authenticate(   username = 'Username' "iv_username'
                                password = 'Password'
                                client   = '900'
                                language = 'E' ).
* set header field for fetching X-CSRF token

  CALL METHOD lo_http_client->request->set_header_field

    EXPORTING

      name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

      value = 'Fetch'.

 lo_http_client->send(

      EXCEPTIONS

        http_communication_failure = 1

        http_invalid_state         = 2 ). "Send the HTTP request

  lo_http_client->receive(

    EXCEPTIONS

      http_communication_failure = 1

      http_invalid_state         = 2

      http_processing_failed     = 3 ). "receive the response

****GET x-csrf TOKEN from earlier response

CALL METHOD lo_http_client->response->get_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

  RECEIVING

    value = l_token.

****GET Cookies from earlier response

DATA: lv_cookie TYPE string.
lv_cookie = lo_http_client->response->get_header_field( 'Set-Cookie' ).

data : lv_http_status type i,
       lv_status_text type string.
    " Check for successful response
  lo_http_client->response->get_status( IMPORTING
     CODE   =     lv_http_status             " HTTP Status Code
     REASON =     lv_status_text             " HTTP status description
).

    CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
      EXPORTING
        url                = lv_url
      IMPORTING
        client             = lo_post_http_client.

* create the URI for the client.


  CALL METHOD cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri

    EXPORTING

      request = lo_post_http_client->request

      uri     = lv_url.

* Set X-CSRF- Token in the new request.

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'X-CSRF-Token'

    value = l_token.

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'Cookie'

    value = lv_cookie.

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_header_field

  EXPORTING

    name  = 'Accept'

    value = 'application/json'.


*lo_http_client->set_csrf_token( l_token ) ,  .

* update the HTTP Method

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_method

  EXPORTING

    method = lo_post_http_client->request->co_request_method_post.

****content type

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_content_type

  EXPORTING

    content_type = 'application/json'.

lo_post_http_client->authenticate(   username = 'Username' "iv_username'
                                password = 'Password'
                                client   = '900'
                                language = 'E' ).


l_body = '{"MANDT":"900","ID":"0000000002","CUST_NAME":"POST_METHOD","INV_NO":"0707022780"}'.


* create Body for the HTTP Post request

CALL METHOD lo_post_http_client->request->set_cdata

  EXPORTING

    data = l_body.

lo_post_http_client->send(

    EXCEPTIONS

      http_communication_failure = 1

      http_invalid_state         = 2 ). "Send the HTTP request

lo_post_http_client->receive(

  EXCEPTIONS

    http_communication_failure = 1

    http_invalid_state         = 2

    http_processing_failed     = 3 ). "receive the response

    " Check for successful response
  lo_post_http_client->response->get_status( IMPORTING
     CODE   =     lv_http_status             " HTTP Status Code
     REASON =     lv_status_text             " HTTP status description
).

if lv_http_status = 200.
  BREAK-POINT.
  ENDIF.

l_result = lo_post_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

WRITE : / L_RESULT.

entry.title: Re: BAPI need for Object Dependency
entry.content: Dear Colleagues, We are creating dependencies through Ippe module (while creating nodes together with it via its node creation bapi)- but these dependencies are local (for ippe) and we can not change them through cu02. Is there a way to update dependency syntax of a local dependecy using BAPI ? (rather than cu02) and which bapi would you recommand ? thanks in advance.

entry.content: can you show the code of your action?

entry.content:
We are unable to PGI ODO 81010270

Please process related que in warehouse monitor

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: SAP note 3551764 - GARN: IRS publication 1494 for tax year 2025 has been released.

entry.title: Re: Abap rap unmanaged with custom buttons only.
entry.content:
Method action for modify importing keys for action <entity>~action Result results.

Method action.

Read entities of <entitiy> in local mode

Entity root

All fields with corresponding #(Keys)

Result lt_results

failed lt_failed.

<Processing logic for post function>

Modify entities of <entity> in local mode entity root

Update fields ( field n1, Field n2)

With values #(( for (ln) in lt_result ( %tky = ln-%tky fieldn1 = ln-value, field n2 = value )).

Populate to results.

entry.title: Re: CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content: The only solution is to compare between SAP and Postman. You may check the SAP request via the ICF Client trace (SICF), and you may export a Postman collection (JSON).

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024

entry.title: Re: ABAP on Cloud: Convert base64 string to LRAW
entry.content:
The classes CL_HTTP_UTILITY and CL_WEB_HTTP_UTILITY are both released for Cloud (C1 contract). They both contain the method DECODE_X_BASE64.

 

  class-methods DECODE_X_BASE64
    importing
      !ENCODED type STRING
    returning
      value(DECODED) type XSTRING .
